frameworks.name: cortical feedback / top-down modulation
frameworks.one_line: Motor planning areas feed back to early visual cortex to bias perception

frameworks.name: multivoxel pattern analysis
frameworks.one_line: Decoding intended action category from spatial fMRI patterns

frameworks.name: action-perception coupling
frameworks.one_line: Action preparation enhances perception of action-relevant object features

core_question: Does preparing an action, even without executing it, modulate activity in early human visual cortex?
key_assumptions: MVPA patterns reflect action-specific neural state
Grasping vs pointing recruit dissociable preparatory representations
Early visual cortex can receive non-sensory motor-related signals
manipulations: Action type: grasping vs pointing toward oriented objects
Execution state: action prepared only vs action executed
Region of interest: early visual cortex (V1) vs anterior parietal cortex
key_findings: Grasping vs pointing decodable with >70% accuracy from signals as early as V1
These early visual cortex signals were present even when actions were prepared but not executed
Anterior parietal cortex showed clearest modulation for actually executed movements
interpretations: Action preparation modulates relevant neuronal populations in early visual areas
Cortical feedback from motor planning regions can shape processing in early visual cortex
